description:
Display an interactive node graph canvas for visualizing and editing Unity serialized data. The canvas opens in a separate window where users can view property relationships as nodes and edges, and edit values that get written back to Unity in real-time.

Use this tool when users want to:
- Visually inspect and edit multiple related component properties
- See object reference relationships as a node graph
- Batch-view properties across multiple GameObjects/Components

The spec describes nodes (components/objects with fields) and edges (reference relationships). Each field can be read-only or editable via serialized property path or code mode.

Rules:
- Always include `context.scenePath` when known
- Fields without `update` are read-only
- Use `mode: "serialized"` for standard properties (float, int, string, bool, vector, color, enum)
- Use `mode: "code"` only when SerializedObject cannot handle the property
- `object_ref` and `asset_ref` fields are always read-only in V1
- Provide meaningful `label` for fields (human-readable display name)
- Set `range` for numeric fields when bounds are known (enables slider UI)
- Use edges to show `object_ref` field relationships between nodes

spec.description: Graph specification describing nodes, fields, edges and their Unity data bindings.

nodes.description: Node list. Each node represents a component or object.

update.description: Write-back configuration. Omit for read-only fields.

edges.description: Edge list representing object_ref/asset_ref relationships between nodes.
